what_it_does: Defines a grown story record with metadata, a root line, and three child section descriptors carrying ids, role lists, archetypes, and empty titles.
observations: The meta block declares version "story-grown-1" and a note naming the Bench growth id.
The root line is "We're Turning the Tides into a Kept Record".
The root has three children with ids ending in p1, p2, and p3.
Child roles are grouped as open/argue, show/prove, and plan/people/close.
All child title fields are empty strings.
standouts: The file is a compact narrative scaffold rather than full prose content.
Role arrays define the intended section functions.
The repeated grown id ties meta.note and child ids to the same generated record.
conventions: Uses a top-level meta/root JSON shape.
Uses stable child ids prefixed by the grown record id.
Stores section intent as role arrays instead of paragraph text.
Uses archetype labels to describe section presentation shape.
Includes title keys even when no title text is present.
concerns: All child title fields are empty, so display labels are not provided here.
The meta note contains process labels such as "Bench", "AUTHORED", and "C3" without definitions in this file.
notes: This file is a three-part story skeleton with a root headline, process metadata, and role/archetype assignments for each child section.
questions: What should an empty child title mean at render time?
What concrete behavior or content does each role label imply?
What does the "C3" label in the meta note identify?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it presents the authored content tree produced by the growth process, with metadata and child section structure.
intention_signals: It seems intended to encode a staged story arc from opening argument, through demonstration and proof, into planning, people, and close.
novelty: The file captures story intent through role bundles and archetype labels while omitting body copy and section titles.
contribution: Contributes a compact authored story scaffold that a fused system would keep as narrative structure: root framing, ordered child sections, role assignments, archetype hints, and generation metadata.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON story-grown record defining metadata, a root headline, and three ordered child sections with role arrays, archetype labels, ids, and empty titles.
intention_for_embedding: This was for preserving a generated authored story tree as a lightweight narrative plan with section roles and presentation archetypes.
